close_reason: gt posting assume now validates template existence via LoadPosting() before writing. Fails fast with actionable error.
description: gt posting assume doesnotexist succeeds — writes any string to .runtime/posting without checking if a template can be resolved. Error only surfaces at next gt prime time, silently producing a session with no posting context. Fix: call LoadPosting() during assume and fail fast if no template exists at any resolution level.
title: gt posting assume does not validate posting existence

description:
attached_molecule: bd-wisp-zdv9
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-16T11:50:56Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

ActorString() produces bracket notation (e.g. gastown/crew/diesel[scout]) but nothing calls it for identity propagation. getAgentIdentity(), detectSender(), AgentEnv()'s BD_ACTOR, and GIT_AUTHOR_NAME all build identity strings manually without posting brackets. Result: posting is invisible in mail from-lines, nudge attribution, BD_ACTOR env var, git commits, and the prime [GAS TOWN] identity line.

## What to fix

Wire posting bracket notation into identity surfaces so that when a posting is active, it appears in communication and attribution.

### Code changes needed

1. **`internal/cmd/prime_session.go` — `getAgentIdentity()`**: For crew and polecat roles, read `.runtime/posting` via `posting.Read()` and append bracket notation if present. This flows into the `[GAS TOWN] role:` identity line.

2. **`internal/cmd/mail_identity.go` — `detectSenderFromRole()`**: For crew and polecat cases, read `.runtime/posting` and append bracket notation to the returned address. Mail `from:` lines will then show e.g. `gastown/crew/diesel[scout]`.

3. **`internal/config/env.go` — `AgentEnv()`**: For polecat and crew cases, if `cfg.Posting` is set, append bracket notation to `BD_ACTOR` and `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME`. The `AgentEnvConfig` struct already has a `Posting` field — ensure callers populate it.

4. **Verify sling dispatch populates `AgentEnvConfig.Posting`**: Check `internal/cmd/sling_dispatch.go` to ensure the posting flows through to env injection, not just `.runtime/posting`.

### Design doc alignment

Per spec issue #2818, Section 6:
- "Mail **display** includes namespaced posting: `From: myrig/crew/alice[rig:dispatcher]`"
- "Beads `created_by` uses full `ActorString()` including posting bracket"
- Verification item 12: "mail from posted agent includes bracket notation"

Note: Mail **routing** is unchanged — `<rig>/crew/<name>` without brackets. Brackets are display-only.

### Tests required

- Test that `getAgentIdentity()` returns bracket notation when `.runtime/posting` is set
- Test that `getAgentIdentity()` returns plain identity when no posting is set
- Test that `detectSenderFromRole()` includes bracket notation for crew/polecat with posting
- Test that `AgentEnv()` BD_ACTOR includes bracket notation when Posting field is set
- Test that GIT_AUTHOR_NAME includes bracket notation when Posting field is set

### Docs to update

- `docs/concepts/identity.md` — verify examples match implementation (may already be correct)
- `docs/reference.md` — add note about bracket notation appearing in mail from-lines and git author

### Important constraints

- Branch: `feat/postings` on `rileywhite/gastown` (NOT upstream steveyegge/gastown)
- Work dir: `/home/riley/gt/gastown/mayor/rig/` (already checked out to feat/postings)
- Run `go build ./...` and `go test ./internal/cmd/ -run "TestPosting|TestAgent|TestDetectSender|TestCrewList" -count=1` before finishing
- Commit and push to `origin feat/postings` when done
title: Posting bracket notation not propagated to identity surfaces

close_reason: Works correctly when env vars are set (GT_ROLE, GT_RIG, GT_CREW). Original finding was from testing without proper agent env context.
description: After setting 'gt crew post diesel dispatcher', running 'gt posting show' from diesel's directory shows 'No posting assigned' when no session posting is active. Should show the persistent posting as the effective posting (with source indicator like 'config' vs 'session').
title: gt posting show does not display persistent posting

close_reason: Posting column added to gt crew list (with source: session/config) and gt polecat list (bracket notation). JSON output includes posting fields.
description: Section 9 of the design doc (issue 2818) specifies a POSTING column in crew and polecat list output showing the posting name and source (persist/assumed). Neither gt crew list nor gt polecat list includes this column. Example from spec: 'alice   myrig    dispatcher (persist) running'.
title: gt crew list / gt polecat list missing posting column

close_reason: Added --reason flag to gt posting assume. Logs to stderr for autonomous assumption auditing.
description: Section 3 and 9 of the design doc (issue 2818) specify: gt posting assume dispatcher --reason 'bead X needs triage'. This flag is not implemented. Autonomous assumption protocol requires agents to log why they assumed a posting. The --reason flag should be accepted and logged (to stderr or mail).
title: gt posting assume missing --reason flag for autonomous assumption logging

close_reason: Added comment explaining why hardcoded is intentional (test coverage prevents drift). No code change needed.
description: Returns hardcoded []string{dispatcher, inspector, scout} instead of enumerating postingsFS. Adding a new embedded template requires updating two places. Should read from the embedded filesystem to prevent drift.
title: BuiltinPostingNames() is hardcoded instead of derived from embedded FS

close_reason: Fixed: cycle argument is now optional. With arg = drop+assume, without arg = drop only. Tests added. Spec updated via comment on issue 2818.
description: gt posting cycle requires a posting name argument — it's just drop+assume in one step. The name 'cycle' implies rotating through available postings automatically. Either rename to 'swap'/'switch', or make cycle actually rotate through BuiltinPostingNames() without an argument.
title: gt posting cycle is misnamed — requires argument, doesn't cycle

close_reason: By design: system-reminder tags in posting drop are the intended mechanism for overriding stale posting context in agent sessions (spec Section 4). Humans rarely run this command directly.
description: posting drop prints <system-reminder> tags directly to stdout assuming LLM agent consumption. Human operators see raw XML. Should gate behind agent-detection check or --json flag, consistent with other CLI output patterns.
title: gt posting drop emits raw <system-reminder> XML to stdout
